commit.message:
fix(mc): replace boundary clamping with linear extrapolation to eliminate chunk seams

The smooth MC density grid has gx = CHUNK_SIZE+1 grid points on each XZ
axis so edge cubes have all 8 corners.  The extra boundary point (xi = gx-1,
zi = gz-1) was clamped to the last interior column's surface_y, duplicating
it.  This created a flat shelf at every chunk boundary — the visible
half-voxel ridge between adjacent chunks.

Fix: linearly extrapolate from the last two interior columns to predict the
neighboring chunk's first-column surface height.  For a uniform slope this
prediction is exact; for typical SRTM terrain it nearly eliminates the seam.
Corner point (xi_last, zi_last) averages both extrapolation directions.

Bump: v0.1.69 → v0.1.70

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Svelte 5 component API - remove new keyword

Svelte 5 changed component instantiation:
- Removed: new App({ target })
- Updated: App({ target })

Components in Svelte 5 are now called as functions directly,
not instantiated with the new keyword.

commit.message:
fix: use gh copilot suggest/explain loop instead of bare gh copilot

Bare 'gh copilot' with no args triggers the extension install prompt
in modern gh (2.87.3) because interactive mode requires the old
extension. The built-in only provides suggest and explain subcommands.

Replace with a simple REPL loop: user types a request, launcher calls
gh copilot suggest or gh copilot explain accordingly.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Redesign UI as retro CASIO calculator with Svelte 5 runes

UI/Design Changes:
- Authentic 1980s-90s CASIO calculator aesthetic
- Beige/gray body with LED green display
- Vintage button styling with proper interaction states
- Fully responsive design for mobile, tablet, and desktop
- Mobile-first CSS with breakpoints at 480px and 768px
- Responsive typography using clamp() for fluid scaling
- Gap and padding variables scale across device sizes

Svelte 5 Modernization:
- Converted to Svelte 5 runes for state management
- Use [dollar]state for reactive state (numberInput, cursorPosition)
- Use [dollar]derived.by for computed values (total calculation)
- Removed reactive declarations (old reactive syntax)
- Updated event handlers to use modern oninput syntax
- Better error handling in derived calculations

Code Quality:
- Simplified Button component (removed class-based styling)
- Clean component separation of concerns
- Removed unused dependencies (svelte-preprocess, carbon-components)
- Better code organization and documentation

Responsive Breakpoints:
- Mobile < 480px: 3.2rem buttons, 1.2rem padding
- Tablet 480-768px: 3.8rem buttons, 1.6rem padding
- Desktop >= 768px: 4.2rem buttons, 2.5rem padding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct inverted PushEnvironment mapping in RegisterDeviceToken

notification-service had 1→production, 2→sandbox — exactly backwards.
Proto defines PUSH_ENV_SANDBOX=1, PUSH_ENV_PRODUCTION=2.

Client sends environment=2 for release builds; server was storing
'sandbox' → APNs query filtered by 'production' → no tokens found
→ push notifications silently broken for all production users.

Aligns with auth-service UpdatePushToken which had the correct mapping.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: smart scroll — anchor on load-older, skip auto-scroll when reading history

Three UX improvements to the browser chat UI:

1. Scroll-position anchoring: When loading older messages, the viewport
   stays on the message that was previously at the top. New history
   appears above without any jump.

2. Smart auto-scroll: A scroll listener tracks whether the user is near
   the bottom (within 60px). New messages only trigger scrollIntoView
   when the user is already at the bottom — reading history is never
   interrupted.

3. New-messages pill: When the user is scrolled up and new messages
   arrive, a sticky '↓ N new message(s)' badge appears at the bottom
   of the message area. Clicking it scrolls to latest and clears the
   counter.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: remove public_key_endpoint_test (REST endpoint deleted in REST-2)

The GET /api/v1/users/:id/public-key endpoint was removed as part of the
REST-2 cleanup. Key bundles are now served exclusively via the KeyService
gRPC GetPreKeyBundle RPC.

The gRPC handler passes req.user_id directly to get_prekey_bundle(), so
the security property (returns target user's key, not requester's) is
enforced by the function signature and gRPC contract.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: always re-clone from temp, show bootstrap command on failure

When running from a temp path, always re-clone so re-runs get the
latest scripts instead of stale cached code. This was causing every
fix to be invisible on re-run.

Also updated failure messages to show the bootstrap command instead
of 'run install.ps1' which would re-run the stale cached version.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
netops-4213: Fix WIF display_name exceeding 32 char GCP limit (#4)

* netops-4213: Fix WIF display_name exceeding 32 char GCP limit

Display names now use project_id-(pool|provider) format. If the name
exceeds 32 chars, the trailing random number suffix is stripped first,
then truncated as a last resort. The 23 char threshold ensures room
for the longest suffix (-provider = 9 chars).

commit.message:
fix: harden chezmoi scripts for non-interactive environments

Problems fixed:
- promptStringOnce fails in non-TTY environments (Codespaces, CI)
  → Guard with stdinIsATTY instead of hardcoding environment names
- apt-get update fails when third-party repos have expired GPG keys
  → Warn and continue (apt-get install still verifies signatures)
- mise install fails on GitHub API rate limits
  → Warn with actionable guidance (mise ls --missing / mise install)
- Docker GPG import not idempotent
  → Add --batch --yes flags
- Non-essential tools (Copilot CLI, draw.io) abort entire script
  → Fail gracefully with warnings

Principle: essential tools fail loudly; optional tools warn and continue.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix 36 audit findings: bugs, security, error handling

Bugs fixed (11):
- Critics (Scored, Factual, Binary) now fail-safe on exceptions instead of passing
- QualityGate fails-safe on LLM evaluation failure
- Escalation max_total_attempts properly enforced
- build_loop: pathlib for cross-platform paths, fix always-true condition
- Code generation: fix triple-quote escaping, fix dead builtin path
- orchestrator: initialize _tool_start before try block
- archetypes: case-insensitive agent name matching

Security fixed (10):
- SSRF: browser_tool and webhook_tool import validation at top level (no silent skip)
- Pipe injection: check ALL segments, not just last
- Git args: shlex.quote() on all user input, not just commit messages
- File tool: symlink traversal prevention (check before and after resolve)
- file_tool + sql_tool: closure-based state instead of mutable globals
- Dashboard: XSS prevention via json.dumps() on API_BASE
- API key logging truncated to last 8 chars

Error handling fixed (15):
- domain_analyzer: 6 fallback paths now log warnings
- agent.py: guard empty conversation, log invalid JSON args
- planner.py: JSON parse fallback to single-step plan
- observability: null-check on DB connection
- human.py: webhook poll exceptions logged
- inbound.py: case-insensitive file watching on Windows

All 1,008 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add transparency note about AI-generated code and documentation

- Clarified that all code and documentation was generated by GitHub Copilot
- Noted that human validation has been limited to testing functionality
- Helps set expectations about the nature of the project

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: detect gh copilot as built-in, not extension

gh copilot is now built-in to gh CLI since v2.x. Checking
'gh extension list | Select-String gh-copilot' always returned
false and 'gh extension install' failed with 'copilot matches
the name of a built-in command or alias'.

Fix: use 'gh copilot --version' exit code to detect availability.
Extension install is kept as fallback for older gh versions.

commit.message:
fix(conductor-qa): redirect gemini output to file only; fix color switch

- Tee-Object was writing gemini stdout to the function pipeline, making
  $result a multi-element array; switch produced one color per element,
  crashing Write-Host -ForegroundColor with a concatenated string.
- Use Out-File instead so Invoke-QA returns only the status string.
- Resolve color into a variable before passing to Write-QA.

feat(worktree): clean -B で orphan な nas/* ブランチも削echoecho

worktree が削除済みだがブランチだけ残っている orphan ブランチを
listOrphanNasBranches() で検出し、-B フラグ指定時にまとめて削除する。

- listOrphanNasBranches() を追加: git branch --list nas/* から
  worktree に紐づかないブランチを抽出
- cleanNasWorktrees() の戻り値を CleanResult に変更
  (worktrees, orphanBranches の件数をecho)

fix: remove 'as Admin' from install step 1

Running as administrator causes gh extension install to write to the
admin profile, making Copilot CLI invisible in normal user sessions.
Open terminal normally - no admin required.

commit.message:
feat: add load-older-messages button to browser UI

The WS protocol had no concept of hasMore — the browser UI never knew
there was older history to page through.

Changes:
- loadOlderMessages now returns { messages, hasMore } like loadConversation
- Server sends { type: 'has_more', value } after replaying initial history
- Server handles { type: 'load_more', beforeTimestamp } and responds with
  { type: 'older_messages', messages, hasMore }
- Browser UI: hasMore state, handles has_more/older_messages WS messages,
  shows '↑ Load older messages' button at top of message list
- App.tsx (Ink): uses updated loadOlderMessages return type to correctly
  clear the PgUp indicator when all history is loaded

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
refactor: apply OOP design principles to core design (#8)

* Initial plan

* refactor: apply OOP design principles to core design

- SRP: decompose Castle.create() into _ensure_dirs(), _build_skill_manager(),
  and _build_channels() private helpers — each with a single responsibility
- DRY + Delegation: extract Session._assemble() classmethod shared by
  Session.create() and Session.resume(), eliminating duplicated setup code

  trace recording is isolated from hook dispatch and loop orchestration

commit.message:
fix: use gh extension list to detect copilot CLI, not gh copilot --help

The previous check used 'gh copilot --help 2>&1 | match copilot' which
always matched because the not-installed warning message itself contains
the word 'copilot'. The extension install was silently skipped on every
fresh machine, leaving users with the interactive install prompt at
first launch.

Switch both install.ps1 and test.ps1 to use gh extension list piped
through Select-String 'gh-copilot' which is unambiguous.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
chore: fix security vulnerabilities and bump Backstage to 1.48.4 (#741)

* chore: fix security vulnerabilities and bump Backstage to 1.48.4

- Backstage 1.48.1 → 1.48.4 (fixes @backstage/plugin-scaffolder-backend, @backstage/integration, @backstage/plugin-techdocs-node vulns)
- koa 2.16.1 → 2.16.4 (Host Header Injection)
- fast-xml-parser → 5.3.8 (stack overflow)
- basic-ftp → 5.2.0 (Path Traversal - critical)
- multer → 2.1.1 (DoS vulnerabilities)
- svgo → 2.8.1 (DoS via entity expansion)
- underscore → 1.13.8 (Prototype Pollution)
- rollup → 4.59.0 (Arbitrary File Write via Path Traversal)
- dompurify → 3.3.2 (XSS vulnerabilities)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* chore: fix additional security vulnerabilities

- @tootallnate/once → 3.0.1 (Incorrect Control Flow Scoping)
- immutable → 4.3.8 (Prototype Pollution - high)
- undici → 6.23.0 (unbounded decompression chain - medium)
- jsonpath resolution → ^1.2.0 (fixes Prototype Pollution alert #165 - medium)
- @azure/msal-node in security-metrics-backend: 3.8.5 → 5.0.6

Note: ajv (v6/v8 split incompatibility), minimatch (multiple major version ranges),
elliptic (no patch), @octokit/* (breaking major bumps), jsonpath #171 (no patch
available) cannot be resolved without breaking changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf: replace O(n²) trimToByteLimit with O(n) backwards scan

The original implementation repeatedly joined the entire line array to
check byte size, making it O(n²) in both time and memory. With MBs of
history (~50k lines) this took ~33 seconds, effectively hanging the UI
during startup so hasMore never applied and the ↑ PgUp indicator never
appeared.

New implementation walks backwards from the end accumulating byte counts
in O(n), completing in <1ms regardless of history size. Even-boundary
trimming is preserved and only applied when actual trimming occurs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: eliminate SSR HTML generation — VNext renders everything (#1370)

* refactor: eliminate SSR HTML generation — VNext renders everything (#1369)

Remove 5,700+ lines of server-side HTML generation that duplicated
what the VNext SPA already renders client-side.

DataScaffold.cs (1,271 lines removed):
- BuildFormFields, BuildViewRowsHtml, BuildTreeViewHtml,
  BuildOrgChartHtml, BuildTimetableHtml and all private HTML helpers
- Dead methods: CoerceTimeOnly, TryBuildLookupUrl, ToInputString,
  CountByType, IsHighCardinalityLookup, GetLookupDisplayValue,
  GetCreateDefaultInputString, RenderChildListEditorHtml
- BuildListRows boolean/lookup output simplified to plain text
  (export path strips HTML anyway)

RouteHandlers.cs (1,046 lines removed):
- Dead SSR: BuildViewSwitcher, BuildTimelineViewHtml, GetViewTypeName,
  BuildPageSizeSelector, BuildEnhancedPagination, BuildUrlWithParam,
  BuildSortableColumnHeaders, BuildTableWithSortableHeaders,
  BuildBulkActionsBar, BuildCommandButtonsHtml, BuildFormFieldsWithErrors,
  BuildExportDropdown
- Dead code: ReturnObjectDictionary, ReturnQueryClauseList,
  ExportHierarchicalJson, ExportFlatCsv, ExportMultiSheetZip,
  BuildHtmlTableDocument, BuildRtfDocument, ApplyUserPasswordForImport,
  ApplyPrefillFromQuery, AppendUserPasswordFieldsIfNeeded,
  ApplyUserPasswordIfNeeded, IsEntityAccessible (duplicate of OpenApiHandler)

Tests (3,422 lines removed):
- Deleted 7 pure SSR test files
- Removed SSR test methods from 4 mixed test files

What remains server-side:
- VNext shell/chrome (ServeVNextShell)
- Schema/metadata API endpoints (BuildEntitySchema)
- Data API (JSON CRUD)
- Export: CSV, list rows (BuildListRows, BuildListHeaders)
- Setup wizard, error pages

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove 8 dead methods (108 lines)

Remove unused private/internal methods found via dead code scan:

DataScaffold.cs:
- LoadByIdForType (orphaned helper)
- MapChildInputType (SSR remnant)
- EscapeJs (SSR remnant)

RouteHandlers.cs:
- ViewRowsToArray, PrependIdRow, ConcatArrays, BuildParentValueRow (dead array utils)
- EscapeRtf (unused RTF export helper)

All had exactly 1 occurrence (definition only, zero callers).

* refactor: port dashboards, reports, views and admin forms to VNext SPA

Migrate SSR HTML pages to client-side JS rendering via VNext SPA:

New VNext JS renderers (vnext-app.js +354 lines):
- renderReportsListPage: report listing from GET /api/reports
- renderReportViewPage: parameter form, results table, CSV export
- renderViewsListPage: view listing from GET /api/views
- renderViewExecutePage: view execution with results table

New API endpoint:
- GET /api/reports: JSON listing of all report definitions with
  parameters metadata

SSR routes removed (-350 lines from RouteRegistrationExtensions):
- GET/POST /admin/sample-data (VNext _sample-data page + JSON API)
- GET/POST /admin/wipe-data (VNext _wipe-data page + JSON API)
- GET /dashboards, GET /dashboards/{id} (VNext _dashboards)
- GET /reports, GET /reports/{id} (VNext _reports)
- GET /views, GET /views/{id} (VNext _views)

SSR code removed:
- DashboardHtmlRenderer: RenderAsync + 5 SSR helpers (-176 lines)

- IRouteHandlers: removed 4 SSR interface members

Test cleanup:

- Removed EscapeRtf tests (method was removed)
- Updated route count assertions

Support word boundary assertions in bitnfa engine (#25)

* Initial plan

* Support word boundary assertions (\b / \B) in bitnfa engine

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

* Support word boundary (\b / \B) assertions in bitnfa engine

Add _reduce_to_position_nfa_boundary() which doubles NFA positions into
non-word/word variants and evaluates boundary epsilons via conditional
epsilon closure during transition-mask construction.

Key changes:
- compiler.py: new _reduce_to_position_nfa_boundary() function; compile_nfa()
  now routes to it when has_boundary is set instead of raising ValueError
- test_word_boundary.py: parametrize tests over both dfa and bitnfa engines;
  remove test_bitnfa_rejects_boundary (no longer applicable)
- Golden files: add word_boundary_bitnfa.c and non_word_boundary_bitnfa.c
- test_golden.py / update_golden.py: register new golden entries

Co-authored-by: strimo378 <59825937+strimo378@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: replace hadolint Docker action with direct binary

The hadolint-action@v3 runs hadolint inside a Docker container with
failure-threshold: info (default), which fails silently without
showing actual lint output. Switch to direct binary installation
(v2.12.0) which respects .hadolint.yaml config reliably and shows
clear output on failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Yasser1728 <241481644+Yasser1728@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(worktree): avoid branch -f when target is checked out

When the target branch (e.g. main) is checked out in a worktree,
cherry-pick directly in that worktree instead of using 'branch -f'
which would desync the working tree. Only use the detached HEAD +
branch -f approach when the branch isn't checked out anywhere.

Also refuse cherry-pick if the target worktree has uncommitted changes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: aggressive predictive bus movement for ~60s data delay

- Assume 60s data staleness (was 10-15s) for forward projection
- City bus base speed prior (~30km/h) blended with measured velocity
- Stop-aware speed modulation: decel on approach, dwell, accel on leave
- Generous overshoot allowance (2 polling periods) for continuous motion
- Faster catch-up (τ=600ms) and stronger boost (1.6x) when behind
- Stop dwell penalty in projection to prevent overshooting past stops
- Pass stop coordinate indices through component tree for modulation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(worktree): use detached HEAD for cherry-pick temp worktree

git worktree add fails with 'already used by worktree' when the
target branch (e.g. main) is checked out elsewhere. Fix by creating
the temp worktree with --detach at the target commit, then advancing
the branch ref with 'git branch -f' after cherry-pick succeeds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: pass config path to hadolint-action explicitly

The action runs hadolint inside a Docker container where automatic
config discovery may not find .hadolint.yaml. Explicitly set
config: .hadolint.yaml for both Dockerfile lint steps.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
